extra.reasoning: The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds on all reachable executions. Analysis: The ONLY transition that sets sent:=true is [t2] (on send_attempt), and its guard is `not opted_out`. So sent can only become true while opted_out is false. The ONLY transition that sets opted_out:=true is [t1] (on optout), and that same atomic update also sets sent:=false. Crucially, opted_out is monotonic: no transition ever sets opted_out back to false. Therefore, once opted_out is true, [t2] is permanently disabled, so sent can never be set to true again. Every transition that can fire while opted_out is true ([t1], [t3], [t4]) sets sent:=false (and the disabled [t2] does nothing). Hence in every reachable state where opted_out is true, sent is false. The property is never violated within (or beyond) the 14-event bound.
